title: Ifloga decumbens
intro: Ifloga decumbens, also known as spreading ifloga, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ifloga decumbens include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Ifloga decumbens is commonly prepared as an infusion or a topical cream, and is also used in some herbal remedies, such as salves and ointments, to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Ifloga decumbens is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is native to Australia and New Zealand. This plant is often referred to as the 'Hairy Ifloga' due to its hairy leaves and stems. The classification of Ifloga decumbens is also related to its unique growth habit, which is typically prostrate or decumbent. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Ifloga decumbens shares characteristics with other plants in this family.
